title: 4. Handling escalation pathways and fallback policies

blocks.content: When an agent fails to match a route, or the sub-agent returns an execution failure, the supervisor must execute a fallback policy. This usually means escalating to a human operator or returning a friendly message.

blocks.title: Loop Detection
blocks.content: If two agents continually refer queries to each other, they will create an execution loop. Implement a 'max_redirects' header in agent messages to break loops.

Question 1: How do you prevent infinite redirection loops in multi-agent routing?
- A) Stop executing code.
- B) Track a redirection counter (e.g. max_redirects) in the message envelope.
- C) Enforce database transactions.

Feedback: Option B is correct. Counters prevent loops.
